mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase borrowing costs and compress equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of months where FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.5%, labor market slack signals weakening consumer spending power, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are positive in >70% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 4.5%, or out-of-sample accuracy drops below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate broad-based demand expansion, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 60-day returns in >60% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during periods of stable inflation reflects healthy capacity utilization and corporate earnings growth, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth yields negative 20-day returns when CPIAUCSL month-over-month change is below 0.3%, or predictive power vanishes in high-volatility regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rates above 5.0% represent restrictive monetary policy that sharply curtails credit availability and equity risk appetite, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns remain positive in >65% of months where FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0%, or threshold model fails to beat buy-and-hold benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rates signal robust labor market tightening and wage growth, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of PAYEMS growth and UNRATE decline produces negative 20-day returns in >55% of observations, or interaction effect is indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Declining real GDP growth reduces corporate revenue expectations and dividend payout capacity, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth is followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in >60% of cases, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production indices above 110.0 indicate strong manufacturing output and supply chain efficiency, triggering positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are negative in >65% of months where INDPRO exceeds 110.0, or the threshold signal shows no statistical edge over random walk.
